description: Thieves, assassins, and those who operate outside the law.
jobs: Thief
A quick-fingered shadow slipping through crowds and picking locks for a living.
Burglar
A dark-clad intruder breaking into homes under cover of night to steal valuables.
Pickpocket
Nimble fingers bumping shoulders in a crowd, lifting coins without the mark noticing.
Cutpurse
A razor-palming figure slicing dangling purse strings while passing in the market.
Highwayman
A masked rider ambushing travelers on lonely roads, demanding valuables at sword point.
Bandit
A wilderness raider striking caravans and outlying farms from a hidden camp.
Brigand
A scarred thug running with an armed gang that robs and terrorizes the countryside.
Outlaw
A forest-dwelling fugitive living beyond the law's reach, wanted and on the run.
Fugitive
A name-changing drifter fleeing town to town, always dodging bounty hunters.
Pirate
A cutlass-swinging sea raider boarding merchant ships and plundering cargo for ransom.
Smuggler
A moonless-harbor runner moving contraband past customs in hidden compartments.
Fence
A back-room dealer buying stolen goods at half price and reselling through clean channels.
Assassin
A silent professional ending lives with poison, blade, or garrote for the right coin.
Hired Killer
A blunt instrument accepting open contracts to kill, caring nothing for subtlety.
Poisoner
A gloved hand lacing wine and food with lethal toxins, killing without drawing steel.
Spy
A chameleon infiltrating enemy courts, stealing secrets and sending coded reports.
Double Agent
A two-faced operative feeding intelligence to both sides, playing each against the other.
Saboteur
A shadow behind enemy lines wrecking bridges, poisoning wells, and burning supply wagons.
Arsonist
A lamp-oil-reeking firestarter setting buildings ablaze in the night for coin or spite.
Forger
A steady-handed penman faking noble seals, land deeds, and letters of credit.
Counterfeiter
A mold-casting criminal pressing false coins from cheap metal to pass as genuine.
Con Artist
A silver-tongued deceiver spinning false identities to swindle victims of their gold.
Swindler
A smiling cheat running crooked schemes that trick honest folk out of their savings.
Charlatan
A costume-changing fraud posing as healer or priest, taking money for skills they lack.
Quack
A wagon-side huckster peddling colored water and ground chalk as miracle cures.
Card Sharp
A sleeve-hiding dealer who fleeces gamblers with bottom-dealing and marked cards.
Extortionist
A quiet menace threatening violence or ruin unless payments are delivered on schedule.
Blackmailer
A secret-hoarding schemer demanding hush money to keep damaging truths buried.
Kidnapper
A sack-carrying snatcher of people, holding them until ransom gold arrives.
Slaver
A chain-rattling captor buying and selling human beings to the highest bidder.
Human Trafficker
A hidden-compartment smuggler moving people across borders in wagons and ship holds.
Brothel Keeper
A velvet-curtained operator running a house of paid companionship and collecting the take.
Procurer
A debt-wielding recruiter coercing people into the flesh trade through deception.
Drug Dealer
A back-alley peddler selling poppy milk and dream-dust from beneath a heavy cloak.
Grave Robber
A lantern-lit digger prying open fresh burials to loot jewelry and burial goods.
Body Snatcher
A midnight ghoul stealing corpses from graves and selling them to anatomy-hungry surgeons.
Cattle Rustler
A night-riding thief driving stolen livestock across county lines under dark skies.
Horse Thief
A stable-creeping shadow who rides away on someone else's mount before dawn.
Deserter
A haunted runaway who abandoned their post, now living in hiding to dodge the noose.
Mutineer
A wild-eyed rebel who turned on their captain, seizing control through open revolt.
Rebel
A forest-cellar fighter taking up arms against the ruling order from the shadows.
Revolutionary
A pamphlet-printing agitator plotting to overthrow the government through organized uprising.
Cultist
A hooded whisperer gathering in secret to worship forbidden powers through dark rites.
Heretic
A defiant preacher of condemned beliefs, risking prison or the pyre for conviction.
Dark Cultist
A blood-stained devotee sacrificing and chanting in service to a shunned malevolent deity.
Snake Oil Salesman
A silver-tongued showman rolling into town with a wagon of fake remedies and big promises.
Bone Collector
A sack-dragging scavenger gathering bones from battlefields for necromancers and curio dealers.
Code Breaker
A cipher-obsessed mind cracking intercepted messages into readable intelligence.
Fire Starter
A tinder-fingered specialist lighting fires with practiced speed, whether for hire or compulsion.
